text: What does 'ADB' stand for in Android development?
correctAnswer: Android Debug Bridge

text: Which language is the primary language for Android development today?
correctAnswer: Kotlin

text: What is the entry point of an Android application?
correctAnswer: AndroidManifest.xml

text: Which component in Android is used to display a list of items efficiently?
correctAnswer: RecyclerView

text: What is the purpose of the ViewModel in Android Architecture Components?
options: To persist UI data across configuration changes
correctAnswer: To persist UI data across configuration changes

text: Which design pattern does Android's LiveData implement?
correctAnswer: Observer

text: In Kotlin, what is the difference between 'val' and 'var'?
options: val is a function, var is a class
val is immutable, var is mutable
val is public, var is private
correctAnswer: val is immutable, var is mutable

text: What does the '?.' operator do in Kotlin?
options: Calls a method only if the object is not null
correctAnswer: Calls a method only if the object is not null

text: What is Coroutine in Kotlin?
correctAnswer: A lightweight concurrency framework

text: Which Firebase service stores data as JSON documents in collections?
correctAnswer: Cloud Firestore

text: What is the purpose of 'suspend' in Kotlin?
options: To mark a function that can be paused and resumed by a coroutine
To make a function run on the UI thread
correctAnswer: To mark a function that can be paused and resumed by a coroutine

text: Which layout places children in a single row or column?
correctAnswer: LinearLayout

text: What is an Intent in Android?
options: A messaging object used to request an action from another component
correctAnswer: A messaging object used to request an action from another component

text: What is the lifecycle method called when an Activity becomes visible to the user?
correctAnswer: onStart()

text: What does MVVM stand for?
correctAnswer: Model-View-ViewModel

text: Which method is called when an Activity is about to be destroyed?
correctAnswer: onDestroy()

text: What is the function of 'Gradle' in Android development?
correctAnswer: Build automation and dependency management

text: Which Kotlin keyword is used to create a singleton object?
correctAnswer: object

text: What does the '!!' operator do in Kotlin?
options: Throws NullPointerException if value is null
correctAnswer: Throws NullPointerException if value is null

text: What is Room in Android?
options: An abstraction layer over SQLite for local databases
correctAnswer: An abstraction layer over SQLite for local databases

text: Which class in Android is used to run code in the background?
correctAnswer: All of the above

text: What is 'Data Binding' in Android?
options: Binding UI components directly to data sources in code
correctAnswer: Binding UI components directly to data sources in code

text: What is an APK?
options: A package file format for Android apps
correctAnswer: A package file format for Android apps

text: Which permission is required to access the internet in Android?
correctAnswer: INTERNET

text: What is a Fragment in Android?
options: A modular section of an Activity with its own lifecycle
correctAnswer: A modular section of an Activity with its own lifecycle

text: What does 'lateinit' mean in Kotlin?
options: A lazy property initialized at first access
Allows a non-null var to be initialized after declaration
A property that is never initialized
correctAnswer: Allows a non-null var to be initialized after declaration

text: What is the purpose of onSaveInstanceState() in Android?
options: To preserve UI state before the Activity is killed
correctAnswer: To preserve UI state before the Activity is killed

text: In Kotlin, what is a data class used for?
options: A class that automatically provides equals, hashCode, copy and toString
correctAnswer: A class that automatically provides equals, hashCode, copy and toString

text: What is the difference between Service and IntentService?
options: IntentService runs on a worker thread and stops itself when done
IntentService is deprecated in all APIs
correctAnswer: IntentService runs on a worker thread and stops itself when done

text: Which Jetpack library is used for navigating between screens?
correctAnswer: Navigation Component

text: What does 'minSdkVersion' define in build.gradle?
options: The target version for new features
The minimum Android API level the app supports
correctAnswer: The minimum Android API level the app supports

text: What is the purpose of the AndroidManifest.xml file?
options: To declare app components, permissions and metadata
correctAnswer: To declare app components, permissions and metadata

text: Which method inflates a layout XML into a View object?
correctAnswer: LayoutInflater.inflate()

text: What is the role of the ViewHolder pattern in RecyclerView?
options: To cache view references and avoid repeated findViewById calls
correctAnswer: To cache view references and avoid repeated findViewById calls

text: In Kotlin, what is a lambda expression?
options: An anonymous function that can be passed as a value
correctAnswer: An anonymous function that can be passed as a value

text: What does 'Flow' in Kotlin coroutines represent?
options: A cold stream of multiple async values
correctAnswer: A cold stream of multiple async values

text: Which Firebase Auth method is used to sign in with email and password?
correctAnswer: auth.signInWithEmailAndPassword()

text: What is Dependency Injection (DI)?
options: Adding imports to a Kotlin file
Providing objects a class needs from outside rather than creating them internally
A method to crash-proof your app
correctAnswer: Providing objects a class needs from outside rather than creating them internally

text: What is the purpose of 'companion object' in Kotlin?
options: To create a second instance of a class
To define static-like members tied to a class
correctAnswer: To define static-like members tied to a class

text: Which layout is best for complex UIs without nested layouts?
correctAnswer: ConstraintLayout

text: What does Firestore's 'runTransaction' ensure?
options: Atomic read-modify-write operations that prevent race conditions
correctAnswer: Atomic read-modify-write operations that prevent race conditions
